summary.intent: Fix consistency bugs in the timer component's Reset and SyncProps message handlers, ensuring zero-duration completion and interval re-arming logic matches the behavior already applied in other arms during pass-3.
summary.outcome: Three new fixes were applied to Reset and SyncProps arms, 3 new tests added (59 total passing), spec updated to match, all CI gates passed (4641 lib tests), and the changes were committed and pushed.

repo: The timer component uses a multi-pass Codex review workflow; each pass can surface consistency issues introduced by prior passes.
The spec at spec/components/specialized/timer.md must be kept in sync with implementation changes to Reset and SyncProps arms.
xci-fast is the fast CI gate run locally before pushing; it must pass before commits are pushed.

code.finding: Reset and SyncProps arms must apply zero-duration completion logic and re-arm adapter interval when interval changes mid-run, consistent with other message arms.

workflow: Codex review runs in passes; pass-4 found 3 threads that were consistency follow-ups from pass-3 changes, suggesting iterative review converges over multiple passes.
The sequence is: fix code → build → add tests → run tests + clippy → update spec → run spec gates → run xci-fast → commit and push → reply/resolve PR threads → re-trigger Codex.

friction: Each Codex pass introduces new findings that require another fix-test-push cycle, making the review loop multi-iteration.
open_items: Reply to and resolve the 3 pass-4 PR threads after pushing.
Re-trigger Codex for a potential pass-5 to confirm no further consistency issues remain.
